title: onobrychis gracilis var. longeaculeata
intro: Onobrychis gracilis var. longeaculeata is a plant species in the pea family Fabaceae, commonly known as a type of sainfoin or hairy sainfoin, which is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. The plant's leaves and flowers are also used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and salves, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
